services.name: api-gateway
services.about: api-gateway is owned by the Platform team. It participates in the request path as a layer-0 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: web-bff
services.about: web-bff is owned by the Growth team. It participates in the request path as a layer-0 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: mobile-bff
services.about: mobile-bff is owned by the Growth team. It participates in the request path as a layer-0 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: checkout-api
services.about: checkout-api orchestrates the buy flow: cart pricing, tax, promotions, and payment authorization. It degrades to a 'serve stale, then error' mode when payments-api is unavailable.

services.name: order-orchestrator
services.about: order-orchestrator coordinates order placement, inventory hold, shipping, and ledger writes. Tier-4 because a failure here can drop confirmed orders.

services.name: account-api
services.about: account-api is owned by the Identity team. It participates in the request path as a layer-1 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: catalog-api
services.about: catalog-api is owned by the Catalog team. It participates in the request path as a layer-1 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: search-api
services.about: search-api is owned by the Search team. It participates in the request path as a layer-1 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: cart-service
services.about: cart-service is owned by the Payments team. It participates in the request path as a layer-1 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: payments-api
services.about: payments-api authorizes and captures charges. It calls fraud-detection, the ledger, and tax, and is on the critical checkout path. A connection pool sized for steady-state load is its known capacity ceiling.

services.name: inventory-service
services.about: inventory-service is owned by the Fulfillment team. It participates in the request path as a layer-2 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: pricing-service
services.about: pricing-service is owned by the Catalog team. It participates in the request path as a layer-2 service. See the linked docs for detail.

services.name: review-service

services.name: wishlist-service

services.name: auth-service
services.about: auth-service issues and validates session tokens for every authenticated request across the platform. It is low-traffic relative to its blast radius and is depended on by nearly every user-facing service, which makes it tier-4 despite modest QPS.

failureBehavior.note: renders catalog-only browse mode; hides the buy button
failureBehavior.author: @lena

failureBehavior.note: serves cached authorization for 90s, then returns 503 to the BFF
failureBehavior.author: @priya

failureBehavior.note: rejects the request; no anonymous checkout path exists
failureBehavior.author: @omar

failureBehavior.note: blocks order placement; does NOT retry to avoid double-charge
failureBehavior.author: @dana

failureBehavior.note: proceeds without a fraud score under a 200ms timeout (risk-accepted)
failureBehavior.author: @sam
